title: Tamil Tawa Fish Fry

ingredients.name: seer fish (vanjaram) or king fish, steaks 2 cm thick

subs.name: pomfret (vaaval) whole fish, scored deeply — adjust fry time to 5 min per side
omit for a more standard chilli-forward fry
2 tsp tamarind paste thinned with 1 tsp water
ghee for finishing adds richness; use along with oil for frying

steps.text: Rinse the fish steaks and pat them completely dry with kitchen towel — moisture prevents a good crust. Score each piece twice on both sides with a sharp knife so the marinade penetrates deep.

Mix red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, black pepper, ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice and salt into a thick paste. Rub this marinade generously all over the fish, working it into the scores. Leave at room temperature for 30 minutes — refrigerate if marinating longer.
Heat a flat tawa or cast-iron skillet over high heat until very hot. Add oil and swirl to coat. Lay the fish steaks flat — they should sizzle loudly. Do not crowd the pan; work in batches if needed.

Fry on high heat for 3–4 minutes on the first side without touching, until a deep red-brown crust forms and the fish releases naturally from the pan. Flip carefully and fry the second side for 3 minutes.
Drop the curry leaves directly onto the hot oil around the fish in the last minute of cooking — they will crackle and infuse the oil. Reduce heat slightly and baste the fish with the spiced oil.
Transfer the fish to a plate lined with kitchen paper. Serve immediately with sliced onion, lemon wedges and meen kuzhambu as a side. The crust softens quickly, so serve at once.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: fish → pomfret (vaaval) whole fish, scored deeply — adjust fry time to 5 min per side; lemon → 2 tsp tamarind paste thinned with 1 tsp water; black-pepper → omit for a more standard chilli-forward fry; oil → ghee for finishing adds richness; use along with oil for frying
